task_statement: CR.v3 P3 Easy Building official-route repair. Implement and verify the customer-facing Easy Building surface so public execution is only brick build / support.operator.cli build with preset_task or graph_packet input. Do not create --large or any third route. Repair route-seal gaps found by read-only subagents: support.operator.build / onboard launch helpers must not function as customer execution routes outside brick build, launch_assembled_building must not allow adapter_cwd live-tree bypass for customer-like use, and driver_public_intake_seal wording/checkers must match both official modes. Also repair/prove the hard fan-in QA boundary: in work -> {code-attack-qa, axis-attack-qa, evidence-integrity} -> closure graphs, QA lane transition_concern_evidence must remain observation/local evidence and must not create pre-closure Link reroute/HOLD; closure-synthesis alone may emit/adopt Link-facing transition concerns under declared route policy. Preserve Brick/Agent/Link: Brick owns templates/return shape, Agent owns returned facts, Link owns Movement/carry/fan-in/reroute. Support/checkers are evidence only. Operator note for 0629b: prior 0629a correctly paused at the declared COO gate, then hit agent_incomplete because the operator supplied a disposed/nonexistent worktree as adapter_cwd on resume. This run removes the mid design-to-work COO gate and must also account for sandbox/resume route-seal evidence honestly.

nodes.work_statement: Design the narrow P3 repair plan from current live evidence and the subagent findings. Separate Brick/Agent/Link: Brick route grammar/template authority, Agent performer/return facts, Link fan-in/Movement/reroute ownership. Produce a bounded file plan for route-seal helper classification and hard fan-in closure-owned transition concerns. Do not edit code in this design step.

Implement the P3 Easy Building repair from the design output. Required route-seal behavior: public customer execution remains brick build / support.operator.cli build only, with input modes preset_task and graph_packet. Do not add --large. Do not let support.operator.build, run_goal_approve_entry, launch_assembled_building, assemble, or internal helper calls become a third customer route. launch_assembled_building must reject or otherwise fail closed for caller-supplied adapter_cwd that points at the live repo/customer tree. driver_public_intake_seal profile wording/checker must reflect both official modes. Required hard fan-in behavior: in hard fan-in QA cohorts, QA lane transition_concern_evidence is observation/local evidence only and must not be adopted before closure-synthesis; closure-origin concerns may adopt reroute/HOLD according to declared Link policy. Add focused negative probes, including a dynamic walker fixture that REDs if QA concern creates pre-closure reroute/HOLD, and proves closure-origin adoption/HOLD. Keep return shape/template refs materialized from Brick templates; do not manually inject required_return_shape or carries_forward_fields.

Codex code/regression QA. Inspect the work diff and run focused checks for route seal and hard fan-in behavior. Verify: no --large path returns; graph_packet cannot author template-owned fields; support.operator.build/launch helpers cannot be used as customer third route; launch_assembled_building adapter_cwd live-tree bypass is rejected/fail-closed; dynamic walker does not adopt QA concern before closure; closure-origin concern behavior is proven. Run git diff --check and focused profiles where possible. Do not source-mutate.

Gemini-local axis/evidence QA. Inspect the work and Codex QA evidence. Attack Brick/Agent/Link boundaries: Brick owns graph/preset grammar and template/return materialization; Agent owns QA returned observations only; Link owns Movement, fan-in, concern adoption, reroute, and HOLD lifecycle. Check that support/helper/checker surfaces do not become route authority, Movement authority, success/quality judgment, or source truth. Do not source-mutate. If provider inspection is impossible, return exact not_proven evidence rather than an empty observation.

Evidence-integrity QA. Inspect raw/step-output/diff/checker evidence. Verify that the Building evidence records the official graph route, that helper/internal route claims are not overstated, that checker green is reported only as support evidence, and that proof limits/not_proven include live provider and customer-ready limits. Do not source-mutate.

Codex closure. Fan in design, work, Codex QA, Gemini axis QA, and evidence-integrity QA. Report FORWARD/HOLD/REROUTE/NOT_PROVEN as support evidence only. If any QA lane source-mutated, if helper route is still effectively a third customer route, if hard fan-in QA concerns can still adopt before closure, or if required focused profiles are missing, report HOLD with remaining_delta and exact evidence refs.
